observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Establish an internal policy for the consumption of FOL to (i) ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations; (ii) protect government resources from waste, loss, unauthorized use, and misappropriation; and (iii) utilize these resources in the most cost-effective and efficient manner to achieve the agency’s objectives;

Consider (i) allocating funds for the construction of FOL storage facility; (ii) incorporate proper procurement planning in the Annual Procurement Plan to avoid large volume of procurement process; and (iii) activate the MGSO’s mandate to monitor the consumption of MGB’s supplies including fuel, oil and lubricants; and

Require the concerned LGU personnel to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Consumption of FOL for CY 2023 for audit purposes. Furthermore, we also recommended that Management ensure compliance with the requirements set forth by COA Circular No. 77-61.

observations.reason: The MENRO is waiting for the schedule of the special Municipal SWM Board Meeting.

observations.recommendation:

Direct the MGSO to conduct physical count of all inventory items every semester and prepare the RPCI within the prescribed period as prescribed by the MNGAS for LGUs. Thereafter, require the MGSO and the MAO to reconcile the RPCI and the accounting records of any variance; and

Require the MGSO and the MAO to maintain complete and updated SCs and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended that Management:

Require all Revenue Collecting Officers (RCOs) to remit their collection in full at the close of each business day in accordance with the abovementioned rule;
 
Require the officer concerned to update the SLs of the AOs and reconcile regularly the balances with the GL. The Municipal Accountant should also be required to prepare the necessary adjusting entry for any deficiencies; and
 
Increase the bonding requirements of the RCOs pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to protect the interest of the Municipality.

Further, to ensure accurate and compliant financial reporting, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain accurate and updated SLs for RPT and SET Receivables, which shall be reconciled with the records of the MT and make any necessary adjustments in the books.

Equip the MDRRM Office personnel and volunteer with necessary trainings that will help them in the efficient and effective management of DRRM-related projects and activities.

Coordinate with the PGS to secure the documents that contain the costs of the items donated as needed for the recording thereof.

Send a competent staff/member of the GFPS to training on how to use monitoring tools such as the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and Project Implementation and Management, and Monitoring and Evaluation in monitoring and evaluation of results of GAD PPAs that would aid in assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the LGU gender mainstreaming efforts.

Require the MGSO to maintain Report of LRN, Report on the Physical Count of LRN (RPCLRN), LRN Property Cards and Local Road map in standard format.

Strengthen the control measures to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of CAs by strictly monitoring the period of liquidation and impose sanctions on AOs who are unable to liquidate on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Create IAS/U, under the administrative supervision and control of the Municipal Mayor to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the agency, in compliance with Budget Circular No. 2004-4 to ensure that the government spends within means, invests in the right priorities, and delivers measurable results for greater transparency and accountability;

Adopt the Internal Audit Manual for LGUs as a guide in establishing, maintaining, and operating an IAS/U, pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016; and

We also recommend that the concerned Municipal officials submit a proposal to the SB for the establishment of the IAS/U in the Municipality, including the organizational structure, functional charts, personnel complement, operating budget and funding source.
observations.reason: Lack of Management action.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the unsubmitted DVs/payrolls to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: No cooperation from previous officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (1) cause the immediate deposit of the remaining undeposited cash collections; (2) deposit her collections intact and daily or not later than the next banking day in the ensuing periods; and (2) cease and desist from allowing cash advances/chits/OUs and paying expenses directly from collections to avoid the risk of theft, loss, or misuse and any adverse action in the audit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to record unaccounted transactions and to investigate the variances on the reported balances, make necessary adjusting entries to restore accurate cash balances in the Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the prompt submission of the required Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS), along with supporting schedules to enable a timely review of the accuracy and validity of bank balances and reconciling items.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the Status of its Time Deposit accounts with the closed PPSBI, Naga City Branch (now OFBank) to adequately provide relevant information to its stakeholders;
b. Conduct reconciliation of accounts with PPSBI and enforce all rights and institute legal actions to require OFB to account and return to the coffers of the Municipality the amount of deposits, plus all interest due; and
c. Consider requesting assistance from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas, the government agency mandated to supervise and regulate banks and other financial institutions, for the recovery of the Time Deposit accounts of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide complete documentation and adequate disclosure for the outstanding obligations certified or charged to Accounts Payable so that the correctness and validity of related liability accounts can be verified and established in the audit.

We recommended that Management direct Municipal Accountant to:
a. Cause the thorough review of the Due to BIR account and the religious preparation and maintenance of an appropriate Subsidiary Ledger for each account;
b. Submit the Schedule of Due to BIR and ensure to prepare and submit this to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Establish a system or routine that will be observed by accounting personnel in maintaining the subsidiary ledger and schedule of Payables to ensure that adequate information is captured in those records.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. review, analyze, and reconcile the details of the Other Payables account; and
b. provide complete documentation and adequate disclosure for the outstanding obligations so that the correctness and validity of the account could be verified and established in the audit.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
b. Direct all Accountable Officers to immediately settle their respective Cash Advances through demand letters.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a review of records to identify the details of the unremitted contributions and the noted variance on the balances of the accounts. Depending on the result of the examination, corrective measures such as immediate remittance to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth, or necessary adjustments in the books of accounts be made. Also, ensure accurate remittance of contributions to prevent the recurrence of unremitted balances.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Strictly implement the retention money provisions under Paragraphs 6.1 to 6.2 of Annex E of IRR of RA 9184; and
b. Require contractors to submit a bank guarantee, irrevocable standby letter of credit, or surety bond callable on demand as substitute if contractors will opt to withdraw the retention money upon full payment after completion.
We recommended the Management to assess projects that have been completed for a year and issue a Certificate of Final Acceptance after ensuring compliance with contract specifications.

We respectfully recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Local Engineer to prepare and submit duly Approved List of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader (SA & ATL) at the beginning of the year pursuant to Item No. 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for verification. The same shall be regularly updated during the year in case of additional or new PPAs is to be implemented in that year, otherwise related expenses will be disallowed in audit and legal and administrative proceedings may be commence as evidence may warrant under the circumstances.
b. Promptly comply with the posting requirement thru a standard Project Signboard as designed; and
c. Submit the duly signed Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of Work Accomplishments (SWA) based on the interval of verification (%)
percentage provided for in the Table.

We recommended that Management require the concerned offices/departments to:
a. Implement immediately the various PPAs funded by the NGAs, if still on-going and warranted;
b. Conduct a thorough reconciliation of the accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for balances requiring correction or clarification; and
c. Remit the unexpended balances of completed projects to the respective source agencies or to the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr), whichever is appropriate, the unexpended balances for the completed projects.

We recommended that Management direct:
a. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Financial Statements and LDRRMF Utilization Report to address the ₱937,924.72 discrepancy, and ensure supporting documents are complete and accurate;
b. Municipal Accountant to update the Notes to Financial Statements with current and consistent data for the reporting period, in accordance with the Annex D format of COA Circular 2012-002.; and
c. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Budget Officer and/or the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to reconcile discrepancies in the computation of the unexpended balances for transfer. This includes ensuring that unimplemented Capital Outlay allocations are correctly classified under Continuing Appropriations, thereby supporting the accurate and compliant transfer of funds to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management to ensure the proper and timely implementation of the programs, projects, and activities embodied in the LDRRMFIP by strictly adhering to the relevant provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20 percent EDF to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We also recommended that, if the originally identified projects are no longer feasible for implementation, the concerned officials should reprogram and reclassify the allocated budget to other eligible and implementable projects under the 20% Development Fund (DF) that are in need of additional funding.

We recommended that the Management issue instructions to the Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to ensure that the endorsed GPB is fully integrated into the overall budgeting process to align financial resources with the planned GAD activities.

We also recommended the Management to ensure compliance with the mandatory 5% GAD allocation based on the total Annual Budget.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to review the existing practices in conducting physical count of its PPE and maintaining property and accounting records to address the noted deficiencies.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the IC to conduct a proper and complete physical count of PPE and ensure that all PPE items are physically counted;
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office (MAO) and the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) to maintain complete and proper accounting and property records relative to PPE items; and
Require the concerned officers and employees responsible for completing the donation process for the remaining PPE items scheduled for donation.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the LCE agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue the regular and periodic reconciliation of the CIB balances per books and cashbook against the monthly BS to attain a correct reporting of the said account’s balance in the FS; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the unidentified book reconciling items considered as prior period errors and thereafter make the necessary JEVs to correct the CIB-LCCA balance.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to consider withholding the salary of AOs who were unable to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed timeframe pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Direct the AOs to observe diligently the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 specifically on the handling, utilization, and liquidation of CAs.
We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the LCE agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit copies of the demand letters sent to non-LGU personnel, along with their replies and justifications, for verification and validation by the AT; and
In cases where all administrative remedies have been exhausted and the liquidation of the subject CAs appear remote, apply for request for write-off of these dormant unliquidated CAs accompanied by the necessary documents in compliance with the provisions stated in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MAO to:
Make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the accurate balances of the AP and other affected accounts;
Ensure that the MGB’s financial transactions are faithfully and accurately represented in the FS in compliance with IPSAS 1; and
Adhere strictly to the criteria outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-009 and use the appropriate account titles when recording the MGB’s financial transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Continue the periodic and regular reconciliation of the Cash-Local Treasury account to ensure accurate reporting, and immediately correct/adjust the respective records upon identification of errors during the reconciliation; and
Equally prioritize the investigation of unreconciled prior period balances and identify possible secondary source documents that may contain details of the balances in order to establish accountability.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation and Management agreed to rigorously maximize the utilization of the 20% DF and ensure the timely implementation of PPAs to attain the desired social, economic, and environmental outcomes crucial for improving quality of life.
We also recommended and Management agreed to:
Devise mechanism for the monitoring of the status of implementation of PPAs funded from the FA to barangays sourced from the 20% DF; and
Require the MBO to prepare and maintain the SAAOB for Continuing Appropriations to facilitate monitoring thereof.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to:
Ensure that only those expenditures related to the implementation of DRRM PPAs should be charged against the LDRRMF; and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and Management agreed to:
Prioritize filling vacant plantilla positions and, when necessary, utilize casual or emergency staff hiring practices in accordance with CSC guidelines and other relevant rules and regulations;
Undertake a comprehensive study and analysis of the Municipality’s staffing pattern to develop a reliable HRP. Furthermore, strictly implement Executive Order No. 58, series of 2022, or the internal policy for hiring JO/COS in the Municipality to provide clear guidelines and controls over the hiring of JO/COS personnel; and
Require the MTO, the MAO and the MHRMO to submit DTRs and ARs with complete signatures of the JO personnel, the immediate supervisors, and the agency head to avoid any suspension in audit.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC to adhere to the provisions of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in its procurement activities.
We also recommended and Management agreed to establish specific payment milestones to better align progress billings with actual project completion, reducing discrepancies between physical progress and financial releases.
We likewise recommended and the concerned officials agreed to ensure that the latest quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation are posted in the LGU’s FDP Boards within 20 calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended and Management, through the MSWDO, agreed to revisit and review its existing policies on granting educational assistance, focusing on the beneficiary selection process, to identify potential improvements that enhance support for eligible constituents while ensuring compliance with applicable laws and regulations, safeguarding against loss or misuse of funds, and promoting efficiency, economy, and effectiveness in government operations.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to ensure reliable PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as
follows:
a. Stricly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, and require the
Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the
results of the actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;
b. Adopt the uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the
prescribed format and to include the required information under Sections
5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
c. Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
d. Conduct the required preliminary activities prior to inventory taking;
e. Record the physical count daily in a standard ICF prescribed in Annex A
of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
f. Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs in the
RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the
Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs
(PPE Ledger Cards) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to provide the
Municipal Accountant with a copy of the RPT records for CY 2024 and prior
years to establish the beginning balance for CY 2025. The Municipal
Treasurer also agreed to henceforth, furnish the Municipal Accountant at the
beginning of each year with a certified list of taxpayers with amounts due and
collectible for the current year as their basis for recording the RPT and SET
Receivables in compliance with Sections 19(b) and 20 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF—QRF and MOOE of the MF is properly
recorded in the Trust Fund books at year-end to reflect accurate financial
reporting pursuant to COA Circular 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct
training sessions for accounting personnel to emphasize the importance of
timely and accurate journal entries related to LDRRMF transfers and
compliance with regulatory guidelines.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Immediately complete the reconciliation of all bank accounts and ensure
that all valid reconciling items identified in the BRS are properly adjusted
in the Municipality’s books;
b. Strictly comply with Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011,
which require timely reconciliation and adjustment of the General Ledger
within 10 days from receipt of the bank statements;
c. Establish and enforce internal control procedures, including setting fixed
monthly deadlines for the preparation and review of BRS and requiring
prompt investigation and resolution of all reconciling items; and
d. Coordinate with the concerned offices to expedite the retrieval of
supporting documents and perform the necessary analyses to validate and
clear long-standing reconciling items.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
agreed to trace the differences between the cashbook and the General Ledgers
of the Cash Local Treasury, Advances for Operating Expenses, and Advances
for Payroll accounts. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management
require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly conduct
the quarterly reconciliation between the treasury records and accounting
records in compliance with Section 143 (A.7) of the LTOM.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review and analyze all dormant accounts to determine their validity and
secure/gather all available documents related to these accounts;
b. If a complete review and analysis cannot be performed due to missing
documents and records, he shall then prepare a list of available records
pertaining to the dormant account, indicate the extent of validation
conducted, and attach all documents to the list. A detailed list of all lost
documents or records must be included; and
c. Prepare and submit a detailed report to the Head of Agency
recommending an investigation to determine the cause/s of the missing
documents and identify the official/s and employee/s responsible for the
loss.

We further recommended and it was agreed that if the investigation results
warrant request for authority to write-off from the Commission, the Head of
the Accounting Unit, through the HoA, should file with or through the ATL
and/or SA, depending on the jurisdictional amount, a request for the approval
of COA to write-off or derecognize the dormant accounts from the books. The
request shall be supported by the documents listed in Annex 8 of the Circular.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that only
transactions not falling under any of the specific maintenance and operating
expense accounts are recorded under the Other MOOE account.
Furthermore, we recommended and it was agreed that personnel authorized
to prepare and approve the JEVs be properly instructed to ensure that the
accounts used align with COA Circular No. 2015-009, dated December 1,
2015.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare an
appropriate journal entry to reclassify the taxes as part of the cost of land to
ensure proper asset valuation and compliance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended and the Department Heads of the Municipality agreed to
enhance their monitoring processes for financial transactions to ensure
documents are submitted promptly to the Municipal Accountant for the
preparation and submission of the required financial reports to the audit team.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to post the Notice of
Delinquency in the Payment of RPT at the main entrance of the municipal
hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the
Municipality pursuant to Section 254 of R.A 7160.

We further recommended and Management agreed that once the Notice of
Delinquency for the Payment of RPT is posted, they should utilize the
remedies provided under R.A. No. 7160 to recover delinquent taxes.

We recommend that Management require the PCL to submit all necessary
documentation to support the disbursement of ₱563,304.91, including but not
limited to:
a. Disbursement Vouchers (DVs)
b. Purchase Requests (PRs)
c. Purchase Orders (POs)
d. Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs)
e. Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS)
f. Activity or Project Designs

We further recommend that Management request PCL to explain and justify the
lack of public bidding or the use of any alternative procurement method in the
procurement of subject goods and services, and instruct them to immediately
liquidate the remaining balance of ₱236,695.09.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide the
Municipal Treasurer with the records of unutilized funds under the BGCM so
that the latter can return these funds to the National Treasury in accordance
with Item 3.9 of DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Submit the following documents for the ₱420,000.00 fund transfers:
 Duly executed Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the
Municipality and each recipient Barangay
 Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses detailing the
project objectives, expected outcomes, and budget allocations
b. Strictly adhere to the documentary requirements prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2012-001, as amended by COA Circular No. 2016-002, for all
future fund transfers to Implementing Agencies, including Barangays. All
MOAs must explicitly provide for liquidation requirements, including
timelines, required supporting documents, and sanctions for non-
compliance.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately coordinate with the
concerned Barangays to submit liquidation reports for the ₱420,000.00
fund transfers, and require compliance within a specified timeframe.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to promptly issue formal demand letters to all officers
and employees with outstanding balances, including those who have retired
or transferred to other agencies.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to establish
and implement stricter monitoring and reporting mechanisms to ensure
regular tracking, timely follow-up, and accurate reporting of all outstanding
receivables from officers and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to revert the balance of the
LGSEF from the Due to NGAs to the General Fund and utilize the amount in
accordance with budgetary requirements and general limitations under
Sections 324 and 325, respectively, of R.A. No. 7160, and other existing laws,
rules, and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure that the
collectors remit collections to the Liquidating Officer at the end of the day.
Deposit slips are immediately prepared upon receipt of the collections, and
collections are not mixed and are deposited intact daily or on the next banking
day.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Management rigorously maximize the utilization of the 20% DF and ensure the timely implementation of PPAs to attain the desired social, economic, and environmental outcomes crucial for improving quality of life.

We also recommended and Management agreed to:

Devise mechanism for the monitoring of the status of implementation of PPAs funded from the FA sourced from the 20% DF; and

Require the MBO to prepare and maintain the SAAOB for Continuing Appropriations to facilitate monitoring thereof.
observations.managementAction:
a. Devise mechanism for the monitoring of the status of the implementation of PPAs funded from Financial Assistance sourced from the 20% DF; and

b. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations for Continuing Appropriations to facilitate monitoring thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging administrative expenses to the 20% DF to prevent the fund from being depleted and its intended purpose being undermined. Instead, we recommended that the administrative expenses of a similar nature be charged against the GF under the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) account of the LGU.

We recommended that the MDRRMO include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
	
We also recommended that Management ensure that only those expenditures related to the implementation of DRRM PPAs should be charged against the LDRRMF. Also, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management ensure the timely and efficient implementation of the priority PPAs as planned, and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF within the targeted timeframes. Specifically, we recommended the following measures to address the above cited causes of non-implementation of PPAs:

Strengthen coordination and communication among Municipal DRRM Council (MDRRMC) members to monitor strictly utilization of available LDRRMF and ensure timely implementation of PPAs;

Include monthly review/discussion of utilization of the LDRRMF and the status of implementation of PPAs during the MDRRMC meetings; and

Create effective inventory management of Anti-Rabies Vaccine and other staple vaccines while considering factors such as: maintaining adequate stock level; determining necessary quantity to purchase; and considering lead times to avoid over allocation of funds, which may result in unused inventory and idle funds at the end of the year.

Consider initiating the development or acquisition of a computerized payroll system for a more accurate and efficient processing of the employee’s payroll and related accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of Management initiatives.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, and direct the Accounting and Property Units to reconcile the results of the physical count with the related property and accounting records pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ensure the accuracy of the reported PPE balances in the financial statements.
We further recommended, which the Management also avail the one-time cleansing of non-existing or missing PPE items in accordance with Section 8.0 of the same Circular to ensure that reported PPE balances are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still had not conducted a physical inventory count. Moreover, a one-time cleansing of non-existing or missing PPE items was also not conducted. This recommendation is reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended that the LCE use the CF for confidential expenses and submit the liquidation reports to ICFAU pursuant to COA, DBM, DILG, GCG, and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still had not utilized the confidential fund for confidential expenses.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant create an SL for Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account (LCCA) under the 20% DF and to use the pro-forma accounting entries under Section 111 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1, on the receipt of Share from IRC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed that this audit recommendation had not yet been implemented.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive coordinate with the LDRRM Officer and LDRRM Council in incorporating in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRM Fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Per CY 2023 the LDRRMFIP, the previous years’ LDRRM still had not been incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the Municipal Treasurer with the records of unutilized funds intended for COVID-19 response and the victims of Typhoon Odette so that the latter can promptly return these funds to the SA, in accordance with Section 4.9 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and Section 3.8 of DBM LBC No. 141 dated December 28, 2021.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed that they had not yet complied with the recommendation but had undertaken to comply after the AAR preparation.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the Municipal Treasurer with the records of unutilized PDAF so that the latter could immediately return the same to the Bureau of Treasury, in accordance with the Supreme Court Decision under G.R No. 209251.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed complied with the recommendation but had undertaken to request compliance after the AAR preparation.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to complete the physical count and direct the Accounting and Property Units to reconcile the results of the physical inventory taking with the related property and accounting records in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020- 006 dated January 31, 2020, to ensure the accuracy of the reported PPE balances in the financial statements. Any discrepancies noted in the reconciliation shall be adjusted in accordance with Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of the Circular as a one-time cleansing of the PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still had not conducted a physical inventory count. Moreover, a one-time cleansing of non-existing or missing PPE items was also not conducted. This recommendation is reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this Report.

15. Henceforth, we recommended that Management comply with NTC Memorandum Circular No. 09-08-91 on the guidelines for the sale and purchase of radio communications equipment to avoid disallowance in the audit.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, on all donations received by the Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (1) distribution sheets or any equivalent documents; (2) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (3) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: A report on the donations of all sorts shall be properly accounted for and in compliance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020. Likewise, a copy of the distribution lists or AR receipts on the distribution of the ingredients for the procurements of ₱767,435.60 had not been included therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPT records for CY 2018 and prior years to establish the beginning balance for CY 2019. Henceforth, furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as the basis for recording the RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: The RPT and SET Receivables had not been set up in the eNGAS because the system could not accommodate a large number of SLs for the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
26. We recommended that the MSWDO submit the aforementioned documents in order for us to properly evaluate the utilization of the SFP Fund and to ascertain whether the objectives of the said program were attained pursuant to AO No. 04 dated February 23, 2016.
Henceforth, require the MSWDO to maintain stock cards to record all receipts and issuances of supplies relative to the implementation of the SFP.
observations.managementAction: A review of the distribution to the 23 DCC disclosed that the distribution lists or AR receipts on the distribution of the ingredients for the total procurements of ₱767,435.60 had not been included therein.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned individuals, determine its collectability, and, if warranted, request for write-off from the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2023, the accounts remained dormant and no authority to write-off was requested from COA. Some amounts are still subject for identification.

We further recommended that management demand from concerned officials and employees the immediate settlement of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: According to Management, the cash advance in the Trust Fund pertained to confidential expenses. However, they could no longer locate copy of the documents to determine whether the amount was already liquidated or not.

observations.recommendation: 32. We recommended that Management stop the payment of honoraria to personnel not authorized under the Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998. We further recommended that the concerned BIR personnel, including the approving officials, refund the honoraria received for lack of legal basis.

33. We recommended that the local chief executive, together with the Sanggunian, require the refund of the excess amount granted and discontinue the granting of PEI in excess of the allowable maximum rate of ₱5,000.00 per personnel, as provided for in Section 1.a of E.O. No. 80 dated July 20, 2012, to avoid overpayment.
observations.managementAction: An appeal had been submitted to COA Region Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: 35. We required the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totaling ₱1,260,000.00, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued in this regard. Henceforth, we also enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be cognizant of the pertinent provisions of law on the grant of allowances/incentives and not to certify disbursements which run counter to the intents and purposes of the regulations prescribed by appropriate and concerned government agencies such as the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.managementAction: An appeal had been submitted to COA Region Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: 36. Required the agency head to strictly implement the policies governing the settlement of disallowance and issuance of clearance. As much as practicable, resort to some remedies and sanctions to effect settlement, such as salary deductions to be applied in satisfaction of the indebtedness to the government. Send written notices to the officials and employees who are no longer in the service and determine the liability of officials who issued clearance despite the existence of unsettled disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Persons liable were no longer connected with the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to observe the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to have reliable PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as
follows:
a. Stricly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition
for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the Accountant and
Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the results of the
actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;
b. Adopt the uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the
prescribed format and to include the required information under Section
5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
c. Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
d. Conduct the required preliminary activities prior to inventory taking;
e. Record /docu the physical count daily in a standard ICF prescribed in
Annex A of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
f. Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs in the
RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the
Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs
(PPE Ledger Cards) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to focus on the physical count of local
roads; and
b. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Engineer to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines
on the local roads asset management system pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prioritize the
immediate completion of the reconciliation and adjustment processes and
establish strict internal controls, including setting deadlines for monthly
reconciliations and ensuring timely resolution of reconciling items, pursuant to
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed take steps to
minimize the effect of staff absences on financial statement preparation. This
could involve cross-training personnel to ensure coverage, hiring temporary
staff during busy periods, or re-evaluating the workflow and deadlines to
accommodate unforeseen delays. Additionally, regularly monitoring staff
attendance and addressing the reasons for frequent absences will be
important in preventing future delays in financial reporting.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to trace the differences between the cashbook and General Ledger of the
Cash Local Treasury account.

We further recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly conduct the
quarterly reconciliation between the treasury records and accounting records
in compliance with Section 143 (A.7) of the LTOM.

We recommended and Management agreed to revert to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund the balance of the undocumented A/P with no
valid claims pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer, MPDO, Municipal
Engineer, and Municipal Accountant agreed to review and monitor the
balances of the continuing appropriations under the 20% DF, which shall be
the basis for the Local Finance Committee to recommend to the Local Chief
Executive the reversion of those pertaining to completed projects and those
no longer needed so that the funds may be appropriated and utilized for other
priority development projects.

We further recommended and Management agreed to address the causes of
delay, like late materials delivery, unavailability of the site, and lack of deed
of donation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide the
Municipal Treasurer with the records of the unutilized funds intended for the
victims of Typhoon Odette so that the latter can promptly return these funds
to the National Treasury, in accordance with Section 3.8 of DBM LBC No. 141
dated December 28, 2021.

We recommended and Management agreed to utilize the procured rescue
vehicle exclusively for response and rescue/relief activities in accordance with
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2022-105 dated August 23, 2022.
Otherwise, the LDRRMF should be reimbursed from the general fund for the
cost of the vehicle.

We further recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012,
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013, on the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Ensure that all collections are deposited intact with the AGDB daily or
not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 69(4) of PD No.
1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I;
b. Submit the validated deposit slip for the ₱343,832.80 deposit made on
January 17, 2024; and
c. Stop setting aside ORs at year-end pending the DO’s liquidations to
accommodate the DO’s refund of the excess CAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the processing of the
required documents for the BIR to facilitate the issuance of an eCAR.
Subsequently, these documents should be forwarded to the Register of Deeds
for the annotation of the deed of sale in the Transfer Certificate of Title and the
processing of the new title for the parcel of land purchased by the
Municipality.

2. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant discontinue the practice of recording donations and purchases of laboratory and medical supplies to the "Other MOOE" account. Furthermore, we advised that personnel authorized to approve the JEVs be properly instructed to ensure that the accounts used align with COA Circular No. 2015-009, dated December 1, 2015.

6. We recommended that the LDRRMC pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2014 to 2015 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.

7. We recommended that Management consider, in the preparation of the Municipality’s Annual Plan, PPAs that can be funded under the LCF share, such as health programs, medical assistance and services, and charities of national character. Henceforth, utilize the LCF share aligned with the intentions and purposes of the fund pursuant to Section 4 of EO No. 357, series of 1996, and Section 1 of RA No. 1169, as amended, for the benefit of the constituents.

11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries for items that do not meet the capitalization threshold for PPE. Henceforth, the purchase of supplies that are small enough to be considered PPE shall be recorded by debiting the appropriate inventory account. In recording the corresponding expense accounts, the basis of the Municipal Accountant shall be the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and the original copy of the Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from the Municipal Treasurer or designated Supply Officer.

12. We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms and by using the RIS to request and issue supplies and materials carried in stock and consolidate the same in the RSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate expense accounts in the books for the supplies consumed.
13. We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials as outright expenses and henceforth record purchases and utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

18. We recommended that the officials and personnel involved in said procurement submit an explanation why they should not be held liable for the loss to the government amounting to ₱78,500.00 arising from the purchase of COVID-19 rapid antigen test kits exceeding the ceiling price provided under the DOH Department Circular No. 2021-0323 dated August 6, 2021. We further recommended that, henceforth, officials involved in the procurement of goods and services comply with laws and regulations, particularly R.A. No. 7581 or the “Price Act,” as amended, to protect the interest of the government.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a justification on the necessity of procuring a high-end or expensive brand/model mobile phone, which is considered “unnecessary” under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012. Otherwise, restitute the value of the mobile phone.
20. We further recommended and Management stop referring to brand names in preparing PRs. Instead, mention the relevant specifications and/or performance requirements of the goods requested. Henceforth, we recommended that Management comply with the procurement procedures for SVP provided under the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

21. We recommended that Management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds by submitting the documentary requirements prescribed under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and comply with the documentary requirements on fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: Only 8 barangays left that have not complied the liquidation and submission of necessary supporting documents for the fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommended that Management submit the Authority for the Entitlement of the Hazard Pay in accordance with Section 4.3 DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 dated March 24, 2020 for us to ascertain the propriety and validity of the claim. Further, we recommended that Management require the refund of the daily hazard pay granted to seven personnel who did not physically report for work on certain dates during the quarantine period.

23. We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual exposure to specific hazards and occupational risks, in accordance with DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016. We further recommended that Management submit to the Auditor Certification from the Department of Health to attest that the Agricultural Technologists are considered PHWs within the contemplation of RA No. 7305.

27. We recommended that Management direct the MPDO to exhaust all available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD database as storage for necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.

30. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that: (a) the grant of the initial cash advance for the PCF is supported with authority by the Local Chief Executive and an estimate of expenses; (b) replenishment of the cash advance is made only when the disbursements reach at least 75%, or as the need requires, by submitting a replenishment voucher supported by PCVs and other documents duly summarized in a report of disbursements; and (c) the PCF is not used for regular expenses, such as rentals, subscriptions, utilities and the like.
observations.managementAction: A balance of ₱11,118.53 had not been liquidated at year-end which indicated that the rules and regulations on the use of PCF had not been complied with.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended that Management ensure that the Council performs its functions in accordance with the DILG MC No. 2005-07 dated February 1, 2005.

34. We recommended that Inventory Committee include the count of supplies and materials when it conducts its physical count and that the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer maintain required supply ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, to comply with the prescribed procedures in the preparation of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) so that utilization can be recorded appropriately in the books. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall strictly follow the Perpetual Method of recording its inventory, as provided in the NGAS Manual and recognize inventory accounts for the above supplies immediately recorded as expense.

the MA and MT to require the persons
determined as liable for the disallowed
transactions to settled their accounts
through payment.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MT to (i) strictly deposit all
her collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day and desist from the
practice of accumulating huge amount of cash balances in her custody; and (ii) check
on a daily basis the correctness of the RCD rendered by the LO; (b) require the LO
to (i) check on a daily basis the correctness of the RCD rendered by the COs; (ii)
prepare the RCD based on the duly verified and corrected reports rendered by COs;
and (iii) reconcile the reports rendered by the LO with the RCD prepared by COs;
and (c) comply with the process required by the MNGAS in the reporting or
procedures governing the CLT account.

2.24. We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) MGSO and MA to reconcile
the discrepancies between the amounts recorded in the books vis-a-vis the RCPPE in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; (b) Inventory and Appraisal
Committee to (i) coordinate with the donors in obtaining transfer receipt documents
and/or deed of donation and acceptance by Management to facilitate the recording of
donated PPE in the books of the Municipality; (ii) if no cost is indicated in the deed
of donation, conduct an appraisal of donated PPE pursuant to Item 6.2.12 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 for eventual recording of the MA in the books; and (c) MGSO
to facilitate the immediate disposal of all unserviceable PPE of the Municipality for
eventual derecognition from the books; and (d) MA to derecognize from the books
the unreconciled SL balance of PPE resulting from conversion to NGAS.

3.18. We recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC, through the ME and
the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) properly plan,
monitor, and evaluate all developmental projects to achieve optimal utilization of the
20% DF and timely implementation and completion of planned PPAs; and (b) ensure
the regular monitoring of the status of project implementation so that necessary
interventions such as initiating replacements or alternatives to the infeasible projects
may be introduced at the earliest time; and (c) ensure that the BAC validate the
eligibility of the competing suppliers conducted through shopping.

4.25. We recommended that the LCE: (a) together with the LDRRMC, require the
LDDRMO to (i) optimize the use of the LDRRMF by fully implementing the
planned PPAs contained in the LDRRMFIP specifically on the use of the 70%
Mitigation Fund to mitigate the risks in the event of disasters and calamities; (ii)
properly plan and prepare the LDRRMFIP for the prior year’s balance of the fund
with details of the PPAs for proper monitoring and reporting of the accomplishments
of the Municipality; (b) instruct the MAO to (i) prepare the Monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization within the prescribed period and submit the same to the
LDDRMO and finally to the AT; (ii) submit the lacking supporting documents and
ensure that succeeding disbursements are supported with sufficient documents to
prove their regularity and occurrence; and (c) require the MBO to charge only
eligible and disaster-related expenditures against the LDRRMF.

5.15. We recommended that the LCE through the MENRO: (a) prioritize the
establishment of the Central MRF and to take heed in providing MRFs to the 14
Barangays; (b) embark on effective projects and programs that support the
Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program through re-use, recycling,
and composting activities for an effective solid waste diversion as embodied in its
SWM Plan; (c) submit to the AT the documentary requirements prescribed under
Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ascertain the
accuracy and regularity of the payments for hauling fee.

6.16. We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman and Focal Person of the GFPS,
(a) require the GAD officials concerned to conduct an assessment and study on the
viability of the PPAs for implementation. If found unimplementable, recommend the
implementation of other GAD related projects; and (b) consider the hiring of
additional and capable personnel to assist in (i) developing and updating the
Municipality’s GAD database for effective GAD planning; and (ii) setting measurable
and quantifiable targets vis-à-vis accomplishments.

7.8. We recommended that the LCE require the MHO to designate a focal person
for the COVID-19 Vaccination Program and deploy encoders to focus on the
preparation and updating of reports.

8.9. We recommended that the LCE to direct the MA and MT to (a) strictly comply
with the intact remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid under and over
remittances of taxes withheld; and (b) facilitate the full remittance of taxes withheld
in December 2022 to the BIR.

9.10. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the HRMO and MTO to reconcile
their current and previous years’ records with the Billing Statement of the GSIS to
determine the details of the amount due to GSIS to facilitate the immediate remittance
thereof; and (b) direct the HRMO to update the GSIS with changes in the
employment status of Municipal officials and employees to avoid the occurrence of
unremitted premiums/loan repayments.

10.5. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the HRMO and MTO to reconcile
the discrepancies between the payrolls and billing statement to identify the cause of
the discrepancies, and make a representation with the PhilHealth to correct the same;
(b) MTO to remit the premiums/loan amortization based on the payroll and or
subsidiary/general ledger; and (c) require the HRMO to religiously update the
PhilHealth on the status of officials and employees such those retired/resigned and
newly recruited personnel to avoid discrepancies in the billing statement and payroll.

11.6. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the HRMO and MTO to reconcile
the discrepancies between the payrolls and billing statement, identify and correct the
cause thereof and make representation with the Pag-IBIG for their reconciliation;
(b) require the MTO to remit the premiums/loan amortization based on the payroll
and or subsidiary/general ledger; and (c) require the HRMO to religiously update the
Pag-IBIG on the status of officials and employees to avoid discrepancies in the
billing statement and payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable based on a duly certified list extracted from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC) showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Sec. 20 of the NGAS Manual which shall also serve as Management’s basis in monitoring arrearages as well as benchmark in determining collection efficiency.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials; and establishment of sufficient stock room for proper storage of items of inventories;
GSO-designate/Inventory Committee to prepare and submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Inventories yearly in order to determine the correctness of the recorded inventories in the books of accounts of the Municipality and to ensure that semestral count of Inventory items is promptly conducted and the corresponding report is submitted on time to the Audit Team; and
Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate to maintain supplies ledger cards (SLC) and stock cards (SC), respectively, for each type of inventories and reconcile the accounting and property records periodically to bring these accounts into agreement.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal treasurer to:       (a) deposit her collections intact and daily or not later than the next banking day;         (b) to cease and desist from and paying expenses directly from collections; and                (c) prohibit borrowings and accommodations or cash payment of expenses from collections.

We recommended that the Management:
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the Status of its Time Deposit accounts with the closed PPSBI, Naga City Branch (now OFB) to adequately provide relevant information to its stakeholders;
Enforce all rights and institute legal actions to require OFB to account and return to the coffers of the Municipality the amount of ₱ 4,935,679.98, plus all interest due; and
Consider requesting assistance from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas, the government agency mandated to supervise and regulate banks and other financial institutions, for the recovery of the Time Deposit accounts of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:          (a) prepare bank reconciliation for all funds (current, savings and time deposit accounts) of the LGU; (b) obtain a copy of the bank statement/snap shot of bank balance  and transaction history of all bank accounts from all the depository banks on a monthly basis or as needed to facilitate reconciliation thereof;  and (b) set-up, maintain, and prepare individual BRS and subsidiary ledgers for these bank accounts to avoid understatement of the CIB-LCCA account at any given date.

We recommended that the Management direct the Offices concerned to ensure the full implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF for optimum utilization of the fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects in accordance with the AIP for timely delivery of basic and value-added services and benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that management: (a) direct the MDRRMO to continue to monitor the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs to fully achieve the desired benefits therefrom; (b) maximize utilization of available funds to strengthen its capability to effectively prepare, respond and recover from the adverse effects and impact of natural disasters or calamities; (c) direct all Municipal officials to judiciously plan, allocate and implement PPAs under the 5% LDRRMF solely and strictly for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery purposes to ensure maximum utilization of fund in compliance with existing law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
update completely the certified list of delinquencies as of December 31, 2021 for the RPUs of Barangay Sto. Niño and ensure that the updated list be submitted to the PTO to request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies;
consider assigning specific personnel to focus solely on preparing and updating yearly a certified list of all RPT delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one year in their jurisdiction; prepare a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the PTO on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection; and
conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize collections especially on the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise the taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the Municipal Treasurer, HRMO and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the General Provisions of the GAA and adopt control measures to ensure that the municipal employees’ net take home pay will not be lower than the required minimum amount set by law after deducting statutory contributions and other allowed/authorized deductions; (b) direct concerned Municipal officials to stop approving the loan applications of employees with net take home pay below the minimum balance required by the GAA.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned Office to submit the Contract of Service/Job Order contracts and related documents within the period provided by existing rules and regulations to facilitate timely auditorial review and examination. Inability to submit these documents with the prescribed period shall be a cause for suspension of the transaction in audit, without prejudice to the administrative liability of the erring public officers or employees who are responsible for such failure.

We recommended that Management install proper tarpaulin signboards at project sites to notify the public of the essential features of GPPAs that are being implemented by the LGU in a given year/s in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 203-004 to promote transparency and accountability.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly reconcile the Due to GSIS account and ensure that all premiums/loan payment are fully remitted to the GSIS within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management: (a) instruct the GAD focal person / GFPs to monitor the implementation of the identified PPAs and maximize utilization of the GAD funds to accomplish the purposes/objectives  for which the GAD PPAs were identified  to deliver immediate and optimum benefits to target beneficiaries; (b) ensure that GAD Accomplishment Report are prepared based on the GPB approved by the DILG, and any changes made therein be properly communicated in writing to the oversight body and attach all pertinent documents relative thereto; (c) revisit internal policies and procedures on GAD and implement a well-defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess GAD PPAs responsiveness in relation to gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that Management: (a) prioritize the development of the sanitary landfill; (b) strictly enforce the Mandatory Segregation of Solid Wastes policy of RA 9003 to achieve minimization and reduction of wastes; and (c) reconstitute the MSWMB to conform to the membership requirements of the Act and to optimize the involvement of civil society and other sectors as envisioned in the law.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
Collecting Officer to strictly deposit the collections of the Municipality on a daily
frequency and intact.

We recommended that the LCE commend the MA for complying with the
timely preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and prompt recognition
of adjustments for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank accounts of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the GSO to (a) conduct
annual physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the AT not later than
January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs, Volume 1; (b) pursue the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (c) maintain updated
PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all the agency properties and reconcile and
adjust the noted discrepancies; and (d) undertake disposal proceedings and
derecognize unserviceable properties from the accounting books and property
records upon disposal thereof based on the approved Inventory and Inspection
Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Planning and
Development Council and the Implementing Offices to (a) implement the PPAs
based on their original timeline so as to attain their optimum value of achieving
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and (b) monitor the
status of implementation of PPAs to ensure prompt and timely execution thereof.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Office and the
implementing offices to continue to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF for the
targeted programs/projects/activities and monitor their implementation to
strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to respond to disasters.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to require the complete
documentation of disbursements prior to payment; (b) MSWDO and GSO to submit
the report on the distribution list of food packs and; (c) HRMO and MHO to
submit the certification on the number of frontliners and patients served with meals
during the pandemic.

We recommended that the LCE (a) capacitate the GAD Focal Point System
and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD
perspective in the LGU through trainings, conferences, symposia administered by
the PCW, DILG or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; and (b) require
the GFPS to equip themselves with competencies on the GeRL/HGDG tool and
PIMME to determine how much of the agency’s actual disbursements in
implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to strictly observe
the prompt and intact remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR on or before the
10th day following the collection month to avoid the penalties of non-compliance to
the BIR regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to strictly observe
the prompt and the full remittance of GSIS withheld premiums and government
share on or before the 10th day of the following month in compliance with Section 6
of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to strictly comply
with the regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums and loan
amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to strictly comply
with regulations on withholding and remittance of PhilHealth contributions,
premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to require the
persons determined as liable for the disallowed transactions to settle their accounts
through payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) take an immediate action to address the noted deficiencies by creating a special team that shall undertake (i) the reconciliation of records of the MAO and GSO; (ii) update the PCs and PPELCs; and (iii)  identify the discrepancies in the land account; and (b) require the MAO and MTO to strengthen the Municipality’s property management by maintaining an updated property records to ensure that all government resources are accounted in the books and facilitate regular reconciliation.

We recommended that the LCE  require the  (a) MTO to furnish the MAO with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and (b) MAO to review the composition of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts as of year-end and reconcile with the records of the MTO to establish the correct balances of the accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We reiterated that the LCE and Local Development Council (a) efficiently plan the development projects by ensuring that high-impact development projects are prioritized so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be attained; and (b) institute effective planning and budgeting to ensure the implementation of development projects as planned.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with laws, rules and regulations in the submission of accounts which prescribe  that the reports and supporting documents submitted by the accountable officers should be immediately recorded in the books of accounts and submitted to the Auditor  within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC Municipal Accountant to (i) refrain from processing claims with insufficient documentation and require the submission of supporting documents on procurement totaling ₱8,282,403.60; and (b) Municipal Engineer to submit the detailed engineering documents on the three implemented infrastructure projects totaling ₱54,795,764.70 to facilitate the conduct of COA  technical review and inspection of the projects, and to prevent the suspension of payments made.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” all vouchers and their supporting documents after the corresponding checks were signed and issued to the payee in strict compliance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE as Head of the Local School Board (a) include in the SEF Budget only the mandated priority programs and projects; and (b) ensure that all procured instructional materials are part of the list of reference materials prescribed by existing DepEd issuance.

We recommended that the LCE require the ME to (a) comply with the standard design specification of projects to be funded from LGSF-AM in order to ensure optimum use of the local road facility and for road safety; and (b) monitor the implementation of the project to ensure the timely completion and delivery of projects to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangays officials in solid waste management and activate the concerned committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source; (b) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all the seven Barangays which remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (c) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Human Resource Management Officer to (i) clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of JO personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and (ii) identify the needs of each Department so that the proper number of JOs with the  right qualifications are hired and established; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the accomplishment reports for services rendered by JOs are attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of the payments.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned Municipal officials to (a) plan the establishment of a centralized GAD Database to help in identifying emerging gender issues; and (b) prepare a post-assessment evaluation report on implemented GAD projects to determine their success and introduce other gender issues to update the needs for gender equality and women empowerment; (c) assign a GFPS in each department to function as an oversight to ensure that the planned GAD projects are implemented, and only GAD projects included in the GPB are implemented.

through maintenance of updated schedules/subsidiary records on CIP  accounts
to come up with reliable accounting reports.

implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in  recording  of  supplies
and  materials;  (b)  maintain  the  prescribed  records  to  ensure  proper
accounting and promote  better  control  in  the  receipt  and  issuance  of
supplies; and (c) provide a stock  room  for  proper  storage  of  items  of
inventories.

with complete supporting documents to ensure their validity,  propriety  and
legality, before approval for payment, to forestall the issuance of  Notices

with the prioritized programs as provided under Section 100 and  272  of  RA

school buildings and other facilities instead of  investing  funds  in  time
deposits especially that the Municipality is a  service-oriented  government
office; and (b) direct the recovery process of  the  amount  of  P758,637.13
plus interests for two years and subsequently  appropriate  the  amount  for
SEF projects.

We recommended that Management:


      a.  further  intensify  and  involve  the  barangay   governments   in

         laws, rules and ordinances;


      b. comply with the requirements of the law  on  waste  segregation  by

      c. strictly  implement  the  anti-littering  policy  and  require  the
         barangays to formulate and implement an Anti-Littering ordinance at

      d. encourage business establishments to use sustainable  biodegradable
         materials  in  the  conduct  of  their  operations  by  passing  an

      e.  enforce  sanctions  on  violators  of  the  ordinances,  laws  and

      f. ensure that the  existing  Materials  Recovery  Facilities  in  all

      g. cause the closure of the open dump  site,  develop  and  operate  a
         sanitary  landfill  as  a  final  disposal  site  for   solid   and
         eventually, residual wastes of municipality.

We    recommended    that    Management    carefully    assess     the
programs/projects/activities  charged  against  the  MDRRMF,  refrain   from
charging  expenditures  that  do  not  concern  with   disaster   risk
management activities and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No.   2012-
002  and  NDRRMC-DBM-DILG  JMC  Nos.  2013-1  and  2014-1  to  avoid   audit
disallowance.

We  recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  GAD  activities  are
implemented in accordance with the approved GAD  plan  utilizing  the  funds
allotted therefore to  address  gender  issues  within  the  agency  or  its
mandate.
