We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the fund transfers from the LGSF/ADM Program to ensure that the benefits to be derived from the implementation project was fully attained by its constituents.

We recommended that Management require the: (a) GFPS or the MPDO to cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; and (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.

We recommended that Management (a) impose all available legal remedies under R.A. No. 7160 to compel delinquent market stall tenants to settle their unpaid accounts; (b) regularly send demand letters and notice of delinquency to the stallholders concerned; (c) adopt a collection strategy whereby the stallholders would be encouraged to settle their accounts; and (d) instruct the members of the Market Committee to regularly hold meeting together with the stall holders to discuss problems affecting their leased stalls and other matters pertaining to market operations.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period and continue to remit all other collections held in trust due to Other National Government Agencies and Government Owned Controlled Corporations within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the GSO to (a) maintain Property Cards and Real Property Card for land for easy reconciliation of the account;  (b) report all unserviceable property in an Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property and immediately request for their disposal to prevent further deterioration; and (c) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the  carrying amount of  property, plant and equipment retired from active use and held for disposal so as not to mislead users of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the fund transfers from the LGSF/ADM Program to ensure that the benefits to be derived from the implementationof  projectcould be  fully attained by its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Development Council (a) efficiently plan the projects to be implemented out of the Fund by investing in high-impact development projects so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be attained; and (b) continue to comply with the provisions of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 in the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to be compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness by providing funds for LDRRM activities; (b) aim to further equip its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations; and (c) require the MDRRMO to exert effort to develop the data base generation of disaster victims to ensure that they will be immediately provided with assistance during calamities.

We recommended that Management (a) exert effort to address the issue of non-establishment of a MRF for the Municipality in order to minimize the solid wastes disposed at the landfill; (b) provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF and to implement waste segregation at source;  (c) strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign among the barangay residents the no segregation, no collection policy and the no-littering policy; and (d) consider the assignment of personnel from the Municipality to accompany the contractor’s personnel in hauling garbage to the landfill to see to it that the amount claimed by the contractor was accurately reported.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the: (a) GFPS or the MPDO to cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; and (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.

We recommended that Management (a) impose all available legal remedies under R.A. No. 7160 to compel delinquent market stall tenants to settle their unpaid accounts; (b) regularly send demand letters and notice of delinquency to the stallholders concerned; (c) adopt a collection strategy whereby the stallholders would be encouraged to settle their accounts; and (d) instruct the members of the Market Committee to regularly hold meeting together with the stall holders to discuss problems affecting their leased stalls and other matters pertaining to market operations.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period and continue to remit all other collections held in trust due to Other National Government Agencies and Government Owned Controlled Corporations within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management (a) resolve the unreconciled difference of PPE accounts balances with the inventory of properties at the time of actual count; (b) work back on the records of the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the preparation of lapsing schedule of depreciation on all depreciable assets which will become the basis for the provision of allowance for depreciation; and (c) secure the deed of donation on lots donated to the Municipality to facilitate the  titling of lots in order to present fairly the asset and the equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) file a claim against the estate of the late Municipal Treasurer for the remaining collectible of the  Municipality; and (b) henceforth require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned accountable officers to strictly adhere to the applicable laws, rules and regulation affecting cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) and the Municipal District Supervisor of Schools as co-chairmen ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are planned and programmed in the annual school board budget to make certain that the use of the Fund is in accordance with its top priorities; and (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer in collaboration with the Municipal Accountant to desist from charging non-priority expenditures against the SEF, and payment thereof.

We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the Municipal Development Council to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% DF for the attainment of the objectives of the Fund; (b) continue to invest in qualitative developmental projects which correspond as well to its Annual investment Plan; (c) cause the re-appropriation of balance of the fund in the ensuing year to finance developmental projects for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents; and (d) henceforth, include the payment of tipping fees on the Municipality’s allocation for solid waste management program, instead of charging the expenditure from the 20% DF.

We recommended that the LCE enforce  full  compliance  with the provisions of RA No. 9003 by (a) requiring the SWMB to fast-track the completion of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; (b) imposing penalties against those violators of the policy on no segregation, no collection of wastes for strict enforcement of the law; (c)  enjoining  all component Barangays to establish their own MRF; and (d) instructing  the MENRO to  be aggressive in the implementation  of concrete projects for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through source reduction and waste minimization measures in order that cost for tipping fees may significantly reduce.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue to be compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund.

We recommended that the LCE continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the financial assistance under the LGSF-ADM program to assure the attainment of its objective to alleviate the living condition of the Municipality’s constituents.
We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the existing rules and regulations in the withholding and remittance of taxes, premiums, and loans to concerned regulatory NGAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the; (a) Local School Board to ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are planned and programmed in the SEF Annual Budget; (b) Municipal Budget Officer desist from improperly charging non-priority expenditures against the SEF; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments of expenditures are in accordance with the top priorities of the SEF.

We recommended that Management (a) provide adequate and realistic budget for its GAD Plan by including therein only programs/projects/activities that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s rights and promote women’s activities; (b)  require the  Municipal Budget Officer and the GAD Focal Person to desist from charging  non-related  expenditures against the GAD Fund which did not partake of the nature of GAD programs, projects and activities; (c)establish sex disaggregated data to institutionalize and improve GAD planning and budgeting; and (d) enact a GAD Code and implement a comprehensive and sustainable GAD programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  desist from charging non-priority development projects against the 20% Development Fund by  properly planning only  programs, projects and activities that  contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
